summary: Market sentiment turned cautious as equities pulled back, with the S&P 500 and Nasdaq closing lower, dragged by Alphabet and broader megacap tech weakness amid geopolitical uncertainty centered on Iran. The dominant macro driver is the evolving U.S.-Iran nuclear deal, with Trump claiming Tehran agreed to nuclear inspections "into infinity" while Iran publicly denied the assertion, creating a credibility gap that is injecting volatility across energy, currency, and equity markets. Oil dropped roughly $1 as investors focused on Hormuz navigation talks between Oman and Iran, while gold fell 2% as rising rate-hike expectations lifted the dollar to a one-year high, signaling that tighter monetary policy expectations are compounding geopolitical pressures. Key risks investors are monitoring include the fragility of the U.S.-Iran agreement, the difficulty of unwinding complex Iran sanctions, residual Middle East conflict between Israel and Lebanon, and the ripple effects on Asian energy supply chains as refiners outside China find little room for Iranian crude. In the short term, markets are likely to remain headline-driven and volatile, with energy prices and safe-haven assets highly sensitive to any deterioration in Iran nuclear talks, while rate-sensitive tech stocks face additional headwinds from a strengthening dollar and persistent hawkish Fed expectations.
